title: [podman] concurrent podman run --rm removes same container under fedora 31
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
when running `podman run --rm` concurrently sometimes there is an error message about a missing container. I suspect two container runs get the same id and are double deleted


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create a script to start concurrent podman runs
```bash
$ cat start_x_container.sh 
#!/bin/bash
IMAGE=ubuntu:bionic
podman pull ${IMAGE}
date -R
echo "start"
for i in $(seq 0 $1); do
        podman run --rm -t ${IMAGE} date -R &
done
wait
echo "finish"
```

2. start podman concurrently (9 concurrent threads in this example)
```
./start_x_container.sh 8
Trying to pull ubuntu:bionic...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 45d437916d57 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 7ddbc47eeb70 skipped: already exists
Copying blob c1bbdc448b72 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 8c3b70e39044 skipped: already exists
Copying config 7753497586 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
775349758637aff77bf85e2ff0597e86e3e859183ef0baba8b3e8fc8d3cba51c
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:07 +0000
start
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:08 +0000
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:09 +0000
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:09 +0000
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:10 +0000
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:10 +0000
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:10 +0000
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:11 +0000
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:10 +0000
Tue, 26 Nov 2019 14:29:11 +0000
ERRO[0003] Error removing container 10e3c73c817789626f5d3756e060bf7157715e219bda113c24b27570d03ba3ad: container 10e3c73c817789626f5d3756e060bf7157715e219bda113c24b27570d03ba3ad does not exist in database: no such container 
finish
```

**Describe the results you received:**
2 out of 5 times I get one or two error messages like the following
```
ERRO[0003] Error removing container 10e3c73c817789626f5d3756e060bf7157715e219bda113c24b27570d03ba3ad: container 10e3c73c817789626f5d3756e060bf7157715e219bda113c24b27570d03ba3ad does not exist in database: no such container 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
podman should run and destroy containers concurrently without a hiccup

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      crun version 0.10.6

      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.3+dev
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
kvm+qemu virtual machine set-up with virtmanager

comments.body:
They're definitely not getting the same ID. This is almost certainly the cleanup process race.

For `podman run --rm`, we will attempt to remove the container in two ways when it exits: from the main Podman process (`podman run` that is attached to the container), and from the "cleanup process" (a separate Podman spawned when the container exits to handle cleanup). We need the extra cleanup process incase you decide to detach from the container at some point, causing the main `podman run` to exit while the container is still running (the process could also be killed, or suffer an error, etc) - it guarantees that the container will be removed.

Normally, if the two are both active, `podman run` always wins, removing the container first (it's a keyboard-interactive process, so it gets first priority on CPU time from the kernel). Adding `&` to the end removes that special interactive priority, so suddenly it's an even race between the two, and sometimes (maybe even most of the time) `podman run` loses, and we get this error message, which isn't really an error; the container is definitely gone, and I believe that `podman run` will still exit without an error. We ought to consider dropping that to a warning.
It's trying to catch `ErrNoSuchCtr` but it's printing an error including `no such container` which seems very suspicious...
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Looks like @rhatdan fixed this one, closing
